title: Regex boundaries should not be used in a way that can never be matched
description: This rule detects regular expressions that use boundary assertions (such as ^, $, \b, \B) in a way that makes the pattern impossible to match. Such patterns are likely to be errors or misunderstandings in the use of regular expressions, and can lead to bugs or unexpected behavior.
recommendation: Review the regular expression and ensure that boundary assertions are used correctly and do not make the pattern impossible to match.
impact: Patterns that can never match may cause logic errors, missed matches, or wasted computation.

checks.message: Review the regular expression and ensure that boundary assertions are used correctly and do not make the pattern impossible to match.
